description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe featuring two large, symmetrical, crescent-shaped blades made of dark metal. The blades are joined at a central hub, which is robust and slightly pointed at the top, giving the axe a formidable appearance. The handle is cylindrical, straight, and wrapped in a series of segmented, reddish-brown grips, likely made of wood or leather, providing a secure hold. The overall design is utilitarian with no visible decorative engravings or embellishments, emphasizing function over ornamentation. The axe appears well-balanced, with the handle length proportionate to the blade width, suitable for two-handed use. The finish of the metal is matte and slightly rough, while the handle has a grippy, textured surface.
description: A double-headed, dark metal axe with crescent blades and a segmented, reddish-brown gripped handle. The design is robust, functional, and suited for heavy-duty use.
description_view_0: Front view showing both symmetrical crescent blades and the full length of the segmented handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the cylindrical handle.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the double blades and handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and handle grip.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical handle, segmented with reddish-brown grip sections, likely wood or leather, designed for secure two-handed grip.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, symmetrical crescent-shaped blades with a central hub, no beard or poll, functional and robust with no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting, with the blade fitted over the handle and secured tightly.
